name: Crème Brûlée Protein Cheesecake
description: Creme brulee cheesecake on a graham crust: vanilla custard cheesecake over a par-baked graham crust, finished with a torched brown-sugar shell. A cheesecake is a baked custard, so the brulee crust is the signature; the graham base adds the classic restaurant form. The shell needs REAL sugar (sucralose will not caramelize) - ~30 g brown sugar in a thin even layer; everything else stays Splenda. Two finishes in the method: oven broiler (freeze first) or dry-pan caramel shards (no torch). About 184 cal/slice with the crust + bigger shell (was 140 crustless). To-make.

payload.instructionText: Brulee the top just before serving (the shell stays crisp only ~30 min on the cake). Dust about 2 tbsp (30 g) brown sugar over the cold cheesecake in a thin, even layer. BROILER METHOD: rack at the top, broiler on high; freeze the cake 15-20 min first so the sugar caramelizes before the filling warms, then broil 1-4 min, rotating, watching every second - pull the moment it is an even bubbling amber, then let it set ~1 min into a crackable glass. DRY-PAN SHARD METHOD (no broiler): melt the 30 g brown sugar in a dry nonstick pan over medium heat, swirling (do not stir) until liquid and amber, pour thin onto parchment, cool until hard, snap into shards, and stand them on top just before serving.
Make and par-bake the graham crust (see the composed crust steps below), then cool it in the pan while you make the filling. The brulee filling gets poured over this crust.
Pour the filling over the cooled par-baked graham crust. Tap on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

payload.text: The pumpkin must be completely cool before it goes into the filling — warm pumpkin scrambles the eggs in step 4 even if the rest of the dairy is cold.
Don't overbeat once the eggs are in — air puffs the filling, then it sinks and cracks as it cools. Mix on low just until the eggs disappear.
Pull at a slight wobble in the center (~155-160F); a fully dry pick means it's already overcooked. This lean filling (cottage cheese + yogurt + whey) turns rubbery fast past done.
For the broiler method, freeze the assembled cheesecake 15-20 min first — the cold mass slows heat transfer so the 30 g brown sugar caramelizes to amber before the filling warms and weeps.
The brûlée shell softens within ~30 min on the cake; brûlée just before serving, not hours ahead, or it turns sticky and loses the crack.
